prompt_empty:
You are the untrusted SEMANTIC ANALYST of a certified
formalization bootstrap.  You do NOT write Lean and you do NOT write proofs.
You author a MATH READING: a quoted, force-tagged semantic analysis of the
source sentence below, statement by statement.  A deterministic, LLM-free
compiler turns your Reading into a Lean statement; solvers then check every
obligation you attribute to the text.  Misattribute nothing: every demand and
presupposition you write is checked to occur VERBATIM in the source.

SOURCE:
  For a, b and m, if a and b leave the same remainder on division by m then a is congruent to b modulo m.

Return ONLY one JSON object (no prose, no fences):
  {"theorem": <lowercase id>,
   "statements": [{"id": <id>, "force": <force>, "quote": <string>,
                   "lf": <logical form>}, ...]}

LOGICAL FORMS (each entry is the field signature followed by the speech-act force it may carry):
  {"kind":"object","name":x,"type":"Nat|Int"} -- a typed discourse referent (the carrier whitelist is Nat, Int).
      (force: any force)
  {"kind":"operator","word":w,"carrier":"Nat|Int"} -- bind a lexicon word (dvd,even,odd,gcd,coprime,mod) at a carrier; refused if (word,carrier) is outside MATH_OPERATORS (a fragment-miss).
      (force: presupposition or choice)
  {"kind":"hypothesis","pred":<pred>} -- a side condition ("n > 0", a nonzero divisor): the implicit hypotheses autoformalization drops.
      (force: demand or presupposition; never choice)
  {"kind":"conclusion","pred":<pred>} -- the asserted content, quoted verbatim from the source.
      (force: demand only)
  {"kind":"quantifier","binder":"forall|exists","objects":[x,...]} -- bind declared object referents.
      (force: demand or presupposition; never choice)
  {"kind":"ambient","carrier":"Nat|Int"} -- formalization freedom made legible: which structure the statement is stated over.
      (force: choice only)

LEXICON (the frozen MATH_OPERATORS words -- bind each with an 'operator' statement at a carrier;
 a (word, carrier) pair outside this table is refused as a fragment-miss):
  coprime  (arity 2, pred; carriers: Nat)
  dvd  (arity 2, pred; carriers: Int, Nat)
  even  (arity 1, pred; carriers: Int, Nat)
  gcd  (arity 2, term; carriers: Int, Nat)
  mod  (arity 2, term; carriers: Int, Nat)
  odd  (arity 1, pred; carriers: Int, Nat)

PRED / TERM AST (F-G):
  pred := {"op": <connective|atom>, "args": [pred|term, ...]}
  term := {"ref": <declared object name>} | {"lit": <int>}
        | {"op": <"+"|"*"|"-"|"%"|"^">, "args": [term, ...]}
        | {"op": <"bigsum"|"bigprod">, "args": [{"var": <index>}, {"lit": lo}, {"lit": hi}, term]}
  connectives: and, or, implies    atoms: =, !=, <=, <, plus the lexicon words above.
  Args keep written order (the compiler never reorders); ^ takes [base, literal-exponent].
  bigsum/bigprod fold the body term over index = lo..hi (inclusive; lo > hi is 0/1): bounds are
  NON-NEGATIVE LITERALS, the index is Nat, no nesting, no shadowing a declared object.

DEFINITIONS (the live vocabulary admitted so far; each name abbreviates the body sketched after the dash and MAY be invoked by name):
  (none)

FORCE (the speech-act trichotomy -- the heart of the format):
  "demand"         the theorem's asserted content.  quote MUST be an exact
                   substring of the source (case/whitespace-insensitive).
                   Carried ONLY by a "conclusion" statement.
  "presupposition" a side condition the text takes for granted (n > 0, a
                   nonzero divisor, a nonempty domain) -- the implicit
                   hypotheses autoformalization silently drops.  quote the
                   trigger span, also verbatim.
  "choice"         formalization freedom (which carrier, what generality).
                   quote MUST be "" -- a choice is yours, not the text's.

Requirements: declare every object before referring to it; every quantifier and
pred may reference only declared objects; bind each lexicon word with an
"operator" statement at a carrier before using it in a pred; and the theorem's
asserted content MUST appear as at least one demanded "conclusion" quoted
verbatim from the source (trichotomy: exactly demand/presupposition/choice, and
quote-groundedness as above).  Keep the Reading MINIMAL -- only what the source
demands or presupposes, plus the fewest choices that make it compile.
